risk_title: Security concerns affecting mini-grid site implementation
risk_summary: Some mini-grid installation sites are located in areas with security concerns, requiring site replacements and potentially delaying implementation timelines.
evidence_quote: A few problems have been noted: (a) some sites are in areas with security concerns and had to be replaced

risk_title: Corridor development project overlap creating implementation risk
risk_summary: Ongoing government-led corridor development project in Addis Ababa may overlap with and impact existing ADELE project distribution line rehabilitation works, affecting 3 out of 50+ feeders.
evidence_quote: The PIU indicated the risk that 3 feeders out of >50 feeders in AA works may be affected by the on-going corridor project of AA administration.

risk_title: Large-scale procurement risk for health and education facilities electrification
risk_summary: Component 4 procurement for 1,337 public facilities poses substantial risk due to scale, bidder qualification, timelines, and sovereign/country-specific risks, with no opportunity to rebid packages that cannot be awarded.
evidence_quote: This procurement, being a very large single public facilities off-grid procurement, poses substantial risk owing to scale, bidder qualification, timelines, and sovereign/country specific risks.

risk_title: Land allocation compliance with World Bank environmental and social standards
risk_summary: EEU must conduct consultations at all mini-grid sites to verify that local authority land allocation processes comply with World Bank E&S rules before installation can proceed, creating a critical path constraint.
evidence_quote: EEU needs to urgently resolve the E&S conditions – conduct consultations at all sites to confirm that the process followed by the local authorities in allocating land to EEU complies with the WB E&S rules. This is on a critical path as the process needs to be complete before installation.
